grinds.description: Full sole — wide, generous bounce for full swings with a square face.
Standard all-purpose sole with light heel relief.
Mid-sole with heel and toe relief. Versatile — square or open.
Widest sole, highest effective bounce. Soft conditions, sand.
Low bounce, narrow sole. For firm conditions and open-face shots.
Wide sole with heel/toe AND trailing-edge relief. K + versatility.
Tour grind — narrow, low bounce, maximum relief.
Wide sole, full swing focus. Square-face shots, soft conditions.
Medium sole, moderate heel and toe relief. Versatile.
Narrow sole, low bounce. Tight lies and creative shots.
Cavity-back wedge with a forgiving wide sole. Designed for square-face shots from typical lies. NOT for open-face creative work.
Standard sole with light heel and trailing-edge relief.
Narrow sole, low effective bounce. Tight lies, firm conditions.
Wide sole, max bounce for soft conditions and bunkers.
Tour grind — very narrow sole, minimal bounce, maximum relief.
Standard wide sole, full-shot focus.
Narrow sole with heel and toe relief. Tour shotmaker grind.
C-shape sole, cambered for versatility. Open or square.
Widest sole, maximum bounce. Soft conditions, sand specialists.
All-purpose standard sole.
Heel and toe relief for open-face versatility.
Wider sole for soft conditions and bunkers.
Widest sole, highest effective bounce.
Very narrow sole, very low bounce for lob specialists.
Versatile mid-sole for general use.
Narrower sole with relief for tight lies and firm conditions.
Classic Ping Eye2-inspired wider sole for forgiveness.
Tour-preferred narrow sole, minimum bounce.
Wide sole for soft turf and sand.
Versatile mid-sole grind.
Cambered sole with heel/toe relief. Versatile.
Full sole for full swings and square-face shots.
Standard sole with light heel relief — the all-purpose middle.
Heel/toe relief for face manipulation around the green.
Widest sole in the line — the bunker club.
Narrow crescent sole, maximum greenside versatility — low bounce, demands precision.
High-bounce player grind — crescent shape with bounce kept under the strike.
The do-everything sole with light heel/toe softening.
Shallower sole for firm turf and shallow attack angles.
Extra bounce for soft conditions and steep deliveries.
Conventional sole under the expanded high-toe face.
All-Terrain-Versatility sole — heavy relief everywhere so the bounce adapts to the delivery.
Shaved sole for firm turf and pickers.
Heel and toe relieved sole under a full-face groove pattern, built to stay clean when the face is laid wide open.
Putter-adjacent chipper sole — extreme width, designed for a putting stroke from off the green.
Very wide gap-wedge sole with three-tiered bounce.
Maximum-width sand sole — nearly impossible to dig.
Rounded standard sole with light heel relief.
Wide, high-bounce sole for steep swings and soft turf.
Low-bounce thin sole for firm turf and open-face shots.
The classic Eye2 high-toe shape with dish sole — the bunker specialist.
Putter-length chipper at 38.5° — built for a putting stroke from the fringe and light rough.
Heel/toe relief for adaptable bounce — the all-rounder.
Traditional full sole for square-face wedge play.
Wide sole with low leading edge — bunkers and soft lies.
Standard sole, light heel relief.
Wide high-bounce sole for steep swings and soft conditions.
Heel/toe-relieved crescent for open-face play.
Narrow low-bounce blade for firm turf and shallow deliveries.
All-purpose sole with moderate heel relief.
Wide full sole — bunkers, soft turf, steep swings.
Crescent heel/toe relief for open-face manipulation.
Ultra-low bounce specialist for the firmest conditions.
Wide cavity-back wedge sole built to flow from game-improvement iron sets.
Classic tour sole with moderate heel relief, tuned per loft.
